title: Clean up test_maintenance_utils: module-level imports and stronger assertions
analysis: This is a test-only cleanup task targeting fused-memory/tests/test_maintenance_utils.py. The file has 6 test methods, each with an identical inline `from fused_memory.maintenance.utils import _safe_close` import (6 occurrences). All sibling test files (test_reindex.py, test_durable_queue.py, test_cleanup_stale_edges.py, etc.) use module-level imports — this is a clear project convention. Additionally, three tests have weak assertions: (1) test_does_not_raise_when_close_raises only checks no exception propagated but never verifies close was actually called, (2) test_noop_when_close_succeeds_no_warning only checks no warnings emitted but never verifies close was called, and (3) test_logs_warning_with_exc_info only asserts exc_info is not None without verifying the captured exception type. The existing _safe_close implementation (utils.py) already satisfies all stronger assertions — it calls service.close() exactly once and passes exc_info=True to logger.warning. No source code changes needed; this is purely test polish.

prerequisites.description: Run all 6 existing tests in test_maintenance_utils.py to establish a green baseline before making any changes

steps.description: Hoist import to module level: Remove the `from fused_memory.maintenance.utils import _safe_close` line from inside all 6 test method bodies (lines 16, 27, 38, 55, 72, 100) and add a single module-level import `from fused_memory.maintenance.utils import _safe_close` after the `import pytest` line. Verify all 6 tests still pass unchanged.

Strengthen test_does_not_raise_when_close_raises: After the `await _safe_close(service, logger, 'test_context')` call (current line 32), add `service.close.assert_awaited_once()` to verify close was actually attempted, not just that no exception propagated. Verify the test passes.

Strengthen test_noop_when_close_succeeds_no_warning: After the caplog context manager block and the existing warning_records assertion, add `service.close.assert_awaited_once()` to verify close was actually called, not just that no warnings were emitted. Verify the test passes.

Strengthen test_logs_warning_with_exc_info: Change the assertion on line 64 from `assert warning_records[0].exc_info is not None` to `assert warning_records[0].exc_info[0] is ValueError` to verify the correct exception type (ValueError, matching the side_effect on line 57) is captured in the exc_info tuple. Update the assertion message accordingly. Verify the test passes.

design_decisions.decision: All 4 changes applied to a single file in separate atomic steps
design_decisions.rationale: Separating the import refactor from each assertion strengthening allows independent verification. If a stronger assertion unexpectedly fails, the import refactor is already safely committed.

design_decisions.decision: exc_info assertion uses `exc_info[0] is ValueError` (identity check on class)
design_decisions.rationale: Python logging stores exc_info as a 3-tuple (type, value, traceback) from sys.exc_info(). Index [0] is the exception class. Using `is` for identity comparison is idiomatic for type checks and more precise than isinstance, since we want to confirm the exact type, not a subclass.

design_decisions.decision: No implementation (type: impl) steps needed — all steps are type: test
design_decisions.rationale: The existing _safe_close implementation already satisfies all stronger assertions. It calls service.close() exactly once (satisfying assert_awaited_once) and passes exc_info=True to logger.warning (satisfying the ValueError type check). This task is purely test cleanup.

design_decisions.decision: Import placed after `import pytest` and before the class definition
design_decisions.rationale: Following PEP 8 import ordering (stdlib, then third-party, then local) and matching the exact style seen in sibling test files like test_reindex.py and test_cleanup_stale_edges.py.

reuse.what: Module-level import convention from sibling test files
reuse.how: All sibling tests use `from fused_memory.xxx import yyy` at module top level. Apply the same pattern: `from fused_memory.maintenance.utils import _safe_close` after the pytest import.
The first test already uses `service.close.assert_awaited_once()`. Apply the identical assertion to test_does_not_raise_when_close_raises and test_noop_when_close_succeeds_no_warning.
